top_signals.title: Oil Erases Wartime Gains as Hormuz Stockpiles Hit Market
top_signals.summary: Goldman economist Farouk Soussa talks about why the Strait of Hormuz doesn't need to come back to 100% for the full amount of oil from the Gulf to come back into the market, and the longer-term outlook for crude amid the US-Iran negotiations. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: FTSE 100 Live: Stocks to dip, oil falls as Strait of Hormuz outlook improves
top_signals.summary: Welcome back to the City AM liveblog. The price of oil is heading lower this morning as the outlook around the Strait of Hormuz improves. Brent crude – the international benchmark for oil prices – tumbled below $73 per barrel, extending losses for a fourth consecutive session. These moves helped wipe out all the gains [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Trump pledges rapid U.S. response for Venezuela after historic earthquakes kill dozens
top_signals.summary: The swift U.S. offer of assistance reflects a degree of diplomatic realignment between the Trump administration and the Venezuelan interim government.
